pairs.question: What was the full title of the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881?
pairs.answer: The full title of the act was 'An Act to further amend "The Constitution Act"'.

pairs.question: What changes did the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881 make to the Legislative Council of South Australia?
pairs.answer: The act increased the size of the Legislative Council from 18 to 24 members and divided the province into four electoral districts, each electing six members.

pairs.question: How did the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881 address deadlocks between the two houses of parliament?
pairs.answer: The act introduced a process for resolving deadlocks between the two houses of parliament.

pairs.question: What electoral system was replaced by the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881?
pairs.answer: The act repealed section 8 of the Constitution Act and section 3 of the Electoral Districts Act 1872, which had required the Legislative Council to be elected from one electoral district.

pairs.question: When was the special election held as a consequence of the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881, and what was its purpose?
pairs.answer: A special election was held in 1882 for the whole Province of South Australia to elect six new members, increasing the size of the Council from 18 to 24 members.

pairs.question: What were the transition arrangements for the Legislative Council under the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881?
pairs.answer: Members were elected for nine years, with two from each district retiring every three years. At the expiration of three, six, and nine years, the eight longest-serving members elected by the whole Province would retire, and each new district would elect two members to replace them. Quorum was set at nine members.

pairs.question: What act superseded the Constitution Act Further Amendment Act 1881, and what change did it make?
pairs.answer: The Constitution Act Amendment Act 1901 superseded the 1881 act and reduced the size of the Legislative Council from 24 back to 18 members following Australia's federation.
